hero.description: At BLAUTEA, we provide close, respectful care committed to each person's wellbeing and environment. We support children, adolescents, and their families through an individualized approach adapted to their needs and pace.

items.title: Close support
items.description: We provide close, respectful care committed to each person's wellbeing and environment.

items.title: Individualized approach
items.description: We support children, adolescents, and their families through an individualized approach adapted to their needs and pace.

items.title: Scientific evidence
items.description: Our approach is grounded in scientific evidence, integrating up-to-date and validated methodologies.

services.title: Services
services.intro1Suffix: , while adapting to each child to support emotional difficulties, develop social skills, and address other challenges.
services.intro2: We carry out diagnostic assessments, family guidance, and specific interventions, providing tailored and personalized care in
services.assessmentDescription: Individualized diagnostic processes adapted to each child, allowing us to properly guide psychological sessions and design effective interventions for children and adolescents.
services.interventionDescription: Intervention sessions adapted to each child and family's needs, working in a flexible and collaborative way to ensure comprehensive care that supports emotional wellbeing throughout each developmental stage.
interventionList: Individual or group parent guidance sessions
Coordination with schools to provide practical strategies for educational and community settings (CDIAP and CSMIJ)

methodology.summary: Our commitment is based on evidence-based interventions and agreed therapeutic plans that consider both the child's and the family's needs.
methodology.earlyStartDescription: An intervention model that starts from the child's interests and motivation, sets goals, and works on skills across different developmental areas.
methodology.parentDescription: Active family coaching in evidence-based strategies to stimulate play, communication, and social interaction in everyday routines.
methodology.pecsDescription: Picture Exchange Communication System to foster functional and expressive communication.
methodology.scertsDescription: Intervention focused on Social Communication and Emotional Regulation with Transactional Support.
methodology.childhoodImageAlt: Visual representation of growth and emotional wellbeing

team.title: Connect with our psychologists
team.description: We support every process with closeness, professional rigor, and sensitivity.

members.name: Maria Duffo
members.quote: Specialized in autism diagnosis and intervention from childhood to adolescence. Experienced in emotional support, anxiety management, behavior difficulties, and attention and hyperactivity challenges.
experience: Hospital Sant Joan de Déu Barcelona (early stimulation, acute hospitalization, and high-specialization autism unit).
Psychodiagnostic assessment processes from early childhood to age 18.
Therapeutic intervention following the ESDM model and cognitive-behavioral intervention in childhood and adolescence.

members.name: Beatriz Daudén
members.quote: Specialized in early autism care, including both diagnosis and intervention following the ESDM model, as well as active support for families. Trained in PECS implementation.
experience: TEA CARE Mas Casadevall Program (Hospital Sant Joan de Déu Barcelona).
Early evaluation and diagnosis of children with ASD warning signs or neurodevelopmental difficulties.
Therapeutic intervention following ESDM and P-ESDM.

contact.title: Contact us
contact.subtitle: Write or call us describing your reason for consultation, and you will receive a response as soon as possible.
contact.locationText: C/ Granada del Penedès, 19-21, Stair B, Mezzanine 1A, 08006 Barcelona

faq.intro: Find clear answers about how we work. We are available for any other questions you may have.

items.question: What ages do you work with?
items.answer: We work with children and adolescents from 0 to 18 years old. Depending on developmental stage, individual characteristics, and specific needs, we provide personalized care adapted to each stage.

items.question: How long does an assessment process take?
items.answer: The duration of the assessment process depends on both family availability and the child/adolescent psychologist conducting the evaluation. It usually involves five to six visits over about one month.

items.question: How often are intervention visits?
items.answer: The frequency of intervention sessions is adjusted to family availability and the child's specific needs, and may be weekly, biweekly, or monthly.

items.question: How much do sessions cost?
items.answer: Session cost varies depending on duration, service type, and goals for each visit. During the first contact call, we provide a clear and detailed explanation of applicable fees.

items.question: Do parents attend the sessions?
items.answer: Family involvement is highly valued throughout assessment and intervention. In early intervention, parents actively participate in sessions. For adolescents, we adapt to the needs of both the young person and the family context.

items.question: Do you offer sessions in natural settings (home, school)?
items.answer: At the moment, some sessions in natural settings such as home or school are offered occasionally. As the team grows, we will expand services and include more frequent interventions in natural environments.

footer.description: Psychology consultations specialized in autism and child and adolescent neurodevelopment. Commitment, rigor, and sensitivity.
